query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer: **Summary:** No new cases have been reported in Uganda after 21 June 2026. Uganda's total remains at **20 confirmed cases (2 deaths)** and has **not** risen above 20. The ECDC outbreak page (updated 6 July 2026) explicitly states the last confirmed Uganda case was on 21 June with none since, and WHO DON612 confirms 20 Uganda cases within the broader outbreak total. The DRC arm continues to grow rapidly (1,561 cases), but Uganda's count is static.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 5 July 2026, a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths, have been reported by the Ministry of Health in Uganda. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported.

sources.title: Communicable disease threats report, 27 June - 3 July 2026, Week 27
sources.snippet: Weekly bulletin covering 27 June - 3 July 2026, includes updates on Ebola and other threats.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: There have been 580 confirmed deaths in D.R. Congo, and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July.

sources.title: Uganda: Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Disease Outbreak 2026 - DREF Operational Update (MDRUG058)
sources.snippet: An outbreak of Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Disease (BUVD) was declared by the Uganda Ministry of Health on 15 May 2026. The index case was a 59-year-old male from DRC who sought medical care in Kampala.

data_points.metric: Uganda confirmed cases (WHO DON612 total breakdown)
